rationale: In the citing paper, Dellaert and Stremersch (2005) appears only in a grouped citation supporting a broad introductory point that sequential product-configuration choices can be onerous. The 2020 article's hypotheses and tests focus on default acceptance and spillover attenuation, without using the 2005 paper's specific utility-complexity framework, design levers, or empirical approach. Thus the reference serves as general background context rather than a substantive building block.
